commands: jq summaries of output/u1conformal/handoff_model.json, handoff_critique.json, handoff_librarian_preliminary.json, handoff_fopt.json, handoff_pta.json
python - <<'PY' ... derive m_Aprime, beta_lambda, m_rho from PTArcade 1sigma region ... PY
WebSearch: PDG/reviews/community dark photon constraints, DarkCast, beam dump/fixed target, BaBar, LHCb, NA64, BBN/CMB dark photon dark Higgs, light scalar Higgs portal, SN1987A, FIPs workshop
warnings: No collider, beam-dump, SN1987A, BBN, CMB, direct-detection, or indirect-detection exclusion is applied because the required portal couplings, lifetimes, branching ratios, abundances, and temperature history are not available upstream.
The FOPT/PTA input assumes xi = 1 while epsilon and lambda_HP are unspecified and assumed negligible for FOPT; this is a thermal-history consistency issue requiring a backend calculation.
The scalar rho is lighter than 2*m_Aprime throughout the PTArcade 1sigma box, so rho -> A_prime A_prime is closed and visible rho decays are portal controlled.
Dark photon limits are not applied from mass alone; kinetic mixing epsilon and visible/invisible decay assumptions are essential.
approximations: The PTArcade 1sigma region is treated as a rectangular box in v and g_D; parameter correlations were not reconstructed from the chain.
Derived mass ranges use endpoint products with the validated convention m_Aprime = g_D*v and m_rho = sqrt(3*g_D**4/(8*pi**2))*v.
SN1987A applicability is treated qualitatively because the preferred A_prime mass range is mostly above the strongest low-mass supernova region and epsilon is absent.
Theory-validity checks are classified as direct only for perturbativity/anomaly consistency, not as complete FOPT validation.

notes: Portal/kinetic mixing parameters lambda_HP and epsilon are unspecified and assumed negligible for FOPT but are required for many constraints.
Mass endpoints use the rectangular 1sigma box, not a chain-level correlated credible contour.

constraints.name: Visible dark photon resonance searches at e+e- colliders
constraints.description: BaBar-like visible A_prime searches constrain kinetic mixing through e+e- -> gamma A_prime with A_prime -> e+e- or mu+mu-. The preferred mass range overlaps, but the model has no epsilon or A_prime branching/lifetime calculation.
constraints.observable: sigma(e+e- -> gamma A_prime) * BR(A_prime -> e+e-, mu+mu-) with prompt lifetime

conventions.kinetic_mixing: epsilon coupling to electromagnetic current after diagonalization

constraints.applicability: Mass overlap exists; no exclusion can be applied without epsilon and decay table.
applicability_notes: Would become direct after implementing dark photon widths and epsilon prior.

source.title: Search for a dark photon in e+e- collisions at BABAR

constraints.name: LHCb prompt and displaced dimuon dark photon searches
constraints.description: LHCb constrains A_prime -> mu+mu- for prompt-like and long-lived regimes. The prompt search overlaps the full mass box and the displaced low-mass window overlaps the lower edge.
constraints.observable: pp -> A_prime + X, A_prime -> mu+mu- prompt/displaced yield
constraints.applicability: Relevant mass range but lifetime and branching information are missing.
applicability_notes: No point is excluded from mass alone.

source.title: Search for dark photons produced in 13 TeV pp collisions

constraints.name: Visible fixed-target and beam-dump dark photon searches
constraints.description: Electron/proton fixed-target and beam-dump searches can constrain visible A_prime decays in this mass range, but require production and decay probabilities for each geometry.
constraints.observable: target production rate times visible decay probability in detector
constraints.applicability: Mass overlap only; backend recast required.
applicability_notes: Use DarkCast-style machinery once couplings are specified.

source.title: Serendipity in dark photon searches
source.authors: Philip Ilten, Yotam Soreq, Mike Williams, Wei Xue

constraints.name: Invisible dark photon missing-energy searches
constraints.description: BaBar/NA64-style missing-energy searches constrain A_prime production followed by invisible decays. The validated minimal model has no specified invisible final state.

conventions.invisible: requires stable or detector-invisible final states

constraints.applicability: Conditional only; minimal no-fermion model does not define dark matter invisible channels.
applicability_notes: Needs a recast if rho or another state is made detector-invisible.

source.title: Search for Invisible Decays of a Dark Photon Produced in e+e- Collisions at BaBar
source.authors: J. P. Lees on behalf of the BaBar Collaboration

constraints.name: Rare meson and resonance production of visible dark photons
constraints.description: Meson production channels can constrain kinetically mixed vectors. The pi0 channel is closed by mass, while eta/vector/heavy-flavor channels can overlap.
constraints.observable: BR(meson -> gamma A_prime or X A_prime) * BR(A_prime -> SM)

conventions.closed_channel: pi0 -> gamma A_prime closed for m_Aprime > m_pi0

constraints.applicability: Some channels overlap, but a channel-specific recast is required.
applicability_notes: Do not apply pi0 dark photon constraints to this mass box.

source.title: US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017: Community Report

constraints.name: Light dark-Higgs scalar beam-dump and rare-meson searches
constraints.description: The radial scalar rho is in the 29-95 MeV range, relevant for light Higgs-portal scalar searches, but portal mixing is unspecified.
constraints.observable: scalar production through Higgs mixing times visible displaced decay probability

conventions.portal: Higgs portal mixing controls visible scalar phenomenology

constraints.applicability: Mass overlap exists; rate and lifetime are missing.
applicability_notes: rho -> A_prime A_prime is closed throughout the 1sigma box.

source.title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson
source.authors: Anthony Fradette, Maxim Pospelov, Josef Pradler, Adam Ritz

constraints.name: Higgs exotic and invisible decays to dark-sector particles
constraints.description: Dark-sector masses are well below m_h/2, but Higgs exotic rates are controlled by lambda_HP, scalar mixing, and any effective h-A_prime couplings.
constraints.observable: BR(h -> dark sector) and visible/invisible/long-lived final states
constraints.applicability: Kinematically open but not constrained without rates.
applicability_notes: Do not infer Higgs exclusions from kinematics alone.

source.title: Feebly Interacting Particles: FIPs 2022 workshop report

constraints.name: BBN/CMB constraints on dark photon freeze-in and decays
constraints.description: Dark photon mediator-decay cosmology overlaps in mass, but needs abundance, lifetime, temperature ratio, and branching fractions.
constraints.observable: BBN/CMB energy injection from freeze-in-produced A_prime decays

conventions.cosmology: constraints depend on abundance and decay channels, not mass alone

constraints.applicability: Mass overlap but no cosmological observable is computed.
applicability_notes: High priority because portals are assumed negligible for FOPT.

source.title: Cosmological Constraints on Decoupled Dark Photons and Dark Higgs
source.authors: Joshua Berger, Karsten Jedamzik, Devin G. E. Walker

constraints.name: BBN/CMB constraints on dark-Higgs freeze-in and decays
constraints.description: The light rho is in the range where Higgs-portal scalar freeze-in and late decays can affect BBN/CMB, but portal mixing and abundances are missing.
constraints.observable: scalar freeze-in yield and late visible energy injection

conventions.portal: super-renormalizable/Higgs-portal scalar constraints are mixing-angle dependent

constraints.applicability: Mass overlap but cosmological rate/yield absent.
applicability_notes: rho -> A_prime A_prime is closed, increasing importance of portal-induced decays.

source.title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson
source.authors: Anthony Fradette, Maxim Pospelov, Josef Pradler, Adam Ritz

constraints.name: Delta N_eff and dark-radiation thermal history
constraints.description: A dark sector at xi=1 during the FOPT must be evolved to BBN/CMB to test dark-radiation and entropy constraints.
constraints.observable: Delta N_eff and dark-sector energy density at BBN/CMB
constraints.applicability: Cannot be inferred from the FOPT xi=1 input alone.
applicability_notes: Relevant to thermal-history consistency, not applied as an exclusion.

source.title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
source.authors: Kohei Fujikura, Sudhakantha Girmohanta, Yuichiro Nakai, Motoo Suzuki

constraints.name: Consistency of xi = 1 with negligible portals
constraints.description: The FOPT/PTA backend assumes xi=1, but epsilon and lambda_HP are unspecified and neglected for FOPT. Thermal contact must be checked.

conventions.thermalization: xi=1 is an input assumption, not a derived result

constraints.applicability: Consistency check requires a new thermal-rate backend.
applicability_notes: High priority because negligible portals can conflict with thermal contact unless initial conditions enforce it.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
source.authors: Sowmiya Balan, Torsten Bringmann, Felix Kahlhoefer, Jonas Matuszak, Carlo Tasillo

constraints.name: SN1987A dark photon energy-loss and trapping bounds
constraints.description: SN1987A dark photon constraints are strongest at lower masses and depend on in-medium production and trapping. The preferred mass box is mostly heavy for a supernova core.
constraints.applicability: Likely weak for most of the mass box, but a lower-edge check may be useful if epsilon is specified.

source.title: Updated Constraints on Self-Interacting Dark Matter from Supernova 1987A
source.authors: Cameron Mahoney, Adam K. Leibovich, Andrew R. Zentner

constraints.name: Ordinary stellar-cooling constraints
constraints.description: The new particles are much heavier than ordinary stellar-core temperatures, so standard stellar-cooling production is Boltzmann suppressed.
constraints.applicability: Not applicable to standard stellar cooling; supernova treated separately.
applicability_notes: No overlap with keV stellar thermal production.

source.title: Feebly Interacting Particles: FIPs 2022 workshop report

constraints.name: Direct-detection scattering constraints
constraints.description: The validated minimal model has no stable dark matter particle or local density fraction, so direct-detection scattering or absorption bounds are not applicable as stated.
constraints.observable: DM-nucleus/electron scattering cross section or dark photon absorption
available_observables: no DM candidate in model handoff

conventions.DM: direct detection requires a stable local population

constraints.applicability: Not applicable to the minimal no-fermion model unless rho or another state is promoted to dark matter.
applicability_notes: Reopen if a metastable rho dark matter scenario is added.

source.title: Direct Detection Constraints on Dark Photon Dark Matter
source.authors: Haipeng An, Maxim Pospelov, Josef Pradler, Adam Ritz

constraints.name: Indirect detection and late-decay energy injection
constraints.description: Long-lived rho or A_prime populations could inject visible energy, but the model has no computed abundance, lifetime, or branching fractions.
constraints.observable: late-time decay or annihilation power into visible states

conventions.indirect_detection: requires a population of long-lived particles

constraints.applicability: Potentially relevant only if a long-lived abundance exists.

source.title: Dark Higgs Dark Matter
source.authors: Cristina Mondino, Maxim Pospelov, Joshua T. Ruderman, Oren Slone

constraints.name: Perturbativity and tree-level unitarity of dark gauge sector
constraints.description: The preferred g_D range is perturbative, and beta_lambda is small in the Coleman-Weinberg convention.
constraints.observable: g_D and beta_lambda loop expansion size
constraints.applicability: Direct validity check passes over the 1sigma box.
applicability_notes: This is not an experimental exclusion.

constraints.name: Gauge anomaly and degree-of-freedom consistency
constraints.description: The validated model has no new chiral fermions, so there is no new chiral gauge anomaly to cancel; the Higgs mechanism preserves degree-of-freedom counting.

constraints.name: FOPT/PTA theory-validity caveats: percolation, gauge dependence, and wall dynamics
constraints.description: The semi-analytic backend and PTA template give an approximate screen, but wall friction, runaway condition, gauge-independent potential variations, and posterior-level percolation were not fully computed.
constraints.observable: percolation probability, reheating, wall velocity/runaway condition, gauge-dependence variation
applicability_notes: Do not treat PTArcade preference as first-principles FOPT validation.

source.title: Gauge-independent gravitational waves from a minimal dark U(1) sector with viable dark matter candidates

missing_calculations.observable: epsilon-dependent A_prime decay table and lifetime
validation_checks: compare hadronic branching fractions to DarkCast or PDG R(s) tables

missing_calculations.observable: lambda_HP to scalar-Higgs mixing and rho decay table
validation_checks: derive theta_h_rho consistently from mass matrix
check rho partial widths below 2 m_mu and above thresholds
recover xi=1 only when interactions equilibrate or initial condition enforces it
compare H(T) conventions with FOPT backend

missing_calculations.observable: freeze-in/residual yields and BBN/CMB energy injection
Delta N_eff at BBN and CMB

validation_checks: compare to published SN1987A benchmark curves
compare gauge-dependent and gauge-independent approximations where available

missing_calculations.observable: DM relic abundance and scattering cross sections if rho is promoted to dark matter
validation_checks: do not run unless a DM candidate is explicitly defined
